member.name: T:Altura20.Api.Controllers.PermissionsController
member.summary: Manages the Permission leaf nodes of the authorization composite. Permissions are atomic access grants identified by a unique Code (e.g. INVENTORY_READ). They can be assigned directly to Users or grouped inside Roles.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.PermissionsController.#ctor(MediatR.ISender)
member.summary: Manages the Permission leaf nodes of the authorization composite. Permissions are atomic access grants identified by a unique Code (e.g. INVENTORY_READ). They can be assigned directly to Users or grouped inside Roles.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.PermissionsController.GetAll(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all active permissions in the system.
member.remarks: Use this list to pick permission IDs when building or updating a Role or User composite. Only active permissions are returned; deactivated ones are excluded.
member.param: Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: List of active permissions.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.PermissionsController.GetChildPermissions(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the permissions directly assigned to a User or Role.
member.remarks:
The parentId is resolved dynamically: - If it matches an active **User**, the user's direct permissions are returned (does not include permissions inherited via roles). - If it matches an active **Role**, the role's direct permissions are returned (does not recurse into child roles).
To get the fully resolved (flattened) permission set of a user, use the login token which contains all resolved IDs.
member.param: GUID of the User or Role whose direct permissions you want to inspect.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Permissions directly assigned to the given parent.
No active user or role found with the provided ID.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.PermissionsController.Create(Altura20.Application.Features.Permissions.Commands.CreatePermission.CreatePermissionCommand,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a new permission.
member.remarks:
Both code and name must be unique across all permissions. The code is automatically normalized to uppercase and should follow the convention MODULE_ACTION (e.g. INVENTORY_READ, PRODUCTION_WRITE).
**Request body example:** ```json { "code": "INVENTORY_READ", "name": "Read Inventory", "description": "Allows querying lot balances and stock levels" } ```
member.param: Permission data. code and name are required.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Permission created successfully. Returns the new permission.
Validation failed or code/name already exists.

member.name: T:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController
member.summary: Manages Roles, which are composite nodes in the authorization tree. A Role can contain Permissions (leaves) and other Roles (child composites), enabling hierarchical permission grouping with unlimited nesting depth.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.#ctor(MediatR.ISender)
member.summary: Manages Roles, which are composite nodes in the authorization tree. A Role can contain Permissions (leaves) and other Roles (child composites), enabling hierarchical permission grouping with unlimited nesting depth.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.GetAll(System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all root roles with their composite tree resolved up to the requested depth.
member.remarks:
Only **root roles** are returned — roles that are not assigned as a child of any other role. Child roles appear nested under their parents according to depth.
**Depth behavior:** | depth | Result | |-------|--------| | 0 | Root role with its direct permissions. `childRoles` is always empty. | | 1 | Root role + its direct children (each with their own permissions). | | N | Recurses N levels into the composite tree. |
**Response shape at depth=1:** ```json [ { "id": "...", "name": "Admin", "permissions": [{ "code": "USERS_WRITE", ... }], "childRoles": [ { "name": "Supervisor", "permissions": [{ "code": "PRODUCTION_READ", ... }], "childRoles": [] } ] } ] ```
member.param: Number of child role levels to include. Default is 0 (root only).
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: List of root roles resolved to the requested depth.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.GetByName(System.String,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns a single role by its exact name, resolved up to the requested depth.
member.remarks: Name matching is case-sensitive. The depth parameter controls how many levels of child roles are nested in the response (same semantics as GET /api/roles).
member.param: Exact name of the role to find.
Number of child role levels to include. Default is 0.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Role found. Returns the role resolved to the requested depth.
No active role found with the given name.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.GetChildRoles(System.Guid,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the roles directly assigned to a User or Role, resolved up to the requested depth.
member.remarks:
The parentId is resolved dynamically: - If it matches an active **User**, returns the roles directly assigned to that user. - If it matches an active **Role**, returns that role's child roles.
The depth parameter controls how many additional levels of children are nested inside each returned role (same semantics as `GET /api/roles`).
member.param: GUID of the User or Role whose roles you want to inspect.
Number of child role levels to include in each result. Default is 0.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Roles assigned to the given parent, resolved to the requested depth.
No active user or role found with the provided ID.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.Create(Altura20.Application.Features.Roles.Commands.CreateRole.CreateRoleCommand,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates a new role with no permissions or child roles.
member.remarks:
Creates an empty role. Use `PUT /api/roles/{roleId}` to assign its composite, or use the additive `POST /api/roles/{roleId}/permissions` and `POST /api/roles/{roleId}/roles` endpoints.
**Request body example:** ```json { "name": "Warehouse Operator", "description": "Access to inventory intake and stock queries" } ```
member.param: Role data. name is required and must be unique.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Role created. Returns the new role with empty permissions and childRoles.
Validation failed or a role with the same name already exists.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.Update(System.Guid,Altura20.Api.Controllers.UpdateRoleRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replaces the full composite of a role (info + permissions + child roles).
member.remarks:
This is a **full replacement** operation: all existing permission and child role assignments are deleted and rebuilt from the arrays provided in the request. Sending an empty array clears all relations of that type.
**Constraints:** - A role cannot be a child of itself. - All provided permission and child role IDs must exist and be active.
**Request body example:** ```json { "name": "Supervisor", "description": "Operational oversight role", "permissionIds": ["3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"], "childRoleIds": ["7b1e4c2a-8f3d-4a9b-b2e1-1c5d8f7a3b6e"] } ```
The response always returns the role at depth=0 (direct permissions and child role list without further nesting).
member.param: GUID of the role to update.
New role info and composite definition.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Role updated. Returns the role with its new composite at depth=0.
Validation failed, circular reference detected, or invalid IDs provided.
Role not found.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.AssignPermissions(System.Guid,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Additively assigns permissions to a role.
member.remarks:
**Additive** — only the listed permissions are added. Existing permissions are preserved. Permissions already assigned to the role are silently ignored (no duplicate error).
To fully replace the composite, use `PUT /api/roles/{roleId}` instead.
**Request body example:** ```json ["3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6", "9c2b1a7f-4d8e-4b3c-a1f2-5e6d7c8b9a0f"] ```
member.param: GUID of the role to assign permissions to.
Array of permission GUIDs to add.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Permissions assigned successfully.
Role not found or one or more permission IDs are invalid/inactive.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.RolesController.AssignChildRoles(System.Guid,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Additively assigns child roles to a role, building the composite hierarchy.
member.remarks:
**Additive** — only the listed roles are added as children. Existing child roles are preserved. A role cannot be a child of itself (direct self-reference is rejected).
To fully replace the composite, use `PUT /api/roles/{roleId}` instead.
**Request body example:** ```json ["7b1e4c2a-8f3d-4a9b-b2e1-1c5d8f7a3b6e"] ```
member.param: GUID of the parent role.
Array of role GUIDs to nest as children.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Child roles assigned successfully.
Role not found, self-reference detected, or invalid child role IDs.

member.name: T:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UpdateRoleRequest
member.summary: Request body for updating a role's info and composite. All permission and child role assignments are replaced with the provided arrays.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UpdateRoleRequest.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid})
member.summary: Request body for updating a role's info and composite. All permission and child role assignments are replaced with the provided arrays.

member.name: T:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UsersController
member.summary: Manages Users, which are the root composite nodes of the authorization tree. A User can hold direct Permissions (leaves) and Roles (composites). The resolved permission set is the union of all direct permissions and all permissions inherited recursively through the assigned role hierarchy.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UsersController.#ctor(MediatR.ISender)
member.summary: Manages Users, which are the root composite nodes of the authorization tree. A User can hold direct Permissions (leaves) and Roles (composites). The resolved permission set is the union of all direct permissions and all permissions inherited recursively through the assigned role hierarchy.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UsersController.Update(System.String,Altura20.Api.Controllers.UpdateUserRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replaces the full composite of a user (profile info + direct permissions + roles).
member.remarks:
This is a **full replacement** operation: - User profile fields (name, lastName, email) are updated. - All existing direct permission and role assignments are deleted and rebuilt from the arrays provided in the request. Sending an empty array clears all relations of that type.
**Does not update:** username or password — those require separate dedicated flows.
**Constraints:** - Email must be unique across all users. - All provided permission and role IDs must exist and be active.
**Request body example:** ```json { "name": "Juan", "lastName": "Diaz", "email": "juan@empresa.com", "permissionIds": ["3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"], "roleIds": ["7b1e4c2a-8f3d-4a9b-b2e1-1c5d8f7a3b6e"] } ```
The response includes resolved `permissionIds` — the full flattened set from direct permissions plus all permissions inherited through roles.
member.param: Case-sensitive username of the user to update.
New profile info and composite definition.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: User updated. Returns the updated user with resolved composite.
Validation failed, email already in use, or invalid IDs provided.
User not found or inactive.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UsersController.AssignPermissions(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Additively assigns direct permissions to a user.
member.remarks:
**Additive** — only the listed permissions are added. Existing direct permissions are preserved. These are permissions held directly by the user, independent of any role assignment.
To fully replace the composite (permissions + roles together), use `PUT /api/users/{username}` instead.
**Request body example:** ```json ["3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6"] ```
member.param: Case-sensitive username of the target user.
Array of permission GUIDs to add directly to the user.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Permissions assigned successfully.
User not found or one or more permission IDs are invalid/inactive.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UsersController.AssignRoles(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Additively assigns roles to a user.
member.remarks:
**Additive** — only the listed roles are added. Existing role assignments are preserved. Assigning a role makes the user inherit all permissions from that role and its entire child hierarchy.
To fully replace the composite (permissions + roles together), use `PUT /api/users/{username}` instead.
**Request body example:** ```json ["7b1e4c2a-8f3d-4a9b-b2e1-1c5d8f7a3b6e"] ```
member.param: Case-sensitive username of the target user.
Array of role GUIDs to assign to the user.
Propagates cancellation from the HTTP request.
member.response: Roles assigned successfully.
User not found or one or more role IDs are invalid/inactive.

member.name: T:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UpdateUserRequest
member.summary: Request body for updating a user's profile and composite. All direct permission and role assignments are replaced with the provided arrays. Email is stored in lowercase; username and password are not updatable here.

member.name: M:Altura20.Api.Controllers.UpdateUserRequest.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Guid})
member.summary: Request body for updating a user's profile and composite. All direct permission and role assignments are replaced with the provided arrays. Email is stored in lowercase; username and password are not updatable here.

member.name: T:Program
member.summary: Auto-generated public partial Program class for top-level statement apps.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Type)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a type. Example: T:Namespace.Outer+Inner`1 becomes T:Namespace.Outer.Inner`1

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Reflection.PropertyInfo)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a property. Example: P:Namespace.ContainingType.PropertyName or for an indexer P:Namespace.ContainingType.Item(System.Int32)

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Type,System.String)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a property given its container type and property name. Example: P:Namespace.ContainingType.PropertyName

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Reflection.MethodInfo)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a method (or constructor). For example: M:Namespace.ContainingType.MethodName(ParamType1,ParamType2)~ReturnType M:Namespace.ContainingType.#ctor(ParamType)

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.GetTypeDocId(System.Type,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates a documentation ID string for a type. This method handles nested types (replacing '+' with '.'), generic types, arrays, pointers, by-ref types, and generic parameters. The flag controls whether constructed generic type arguments are emitted, while controls whether the generic arity marker (e.g. "`1") is appended.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.NormalizeDocId(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a documentation comment ID to match the compiler-style format. Strips the return type suffix for ordinary methods but retains it for conversion operators.
member.param: The documentation comment ID to normalize.
